files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding debug:bool=False + debug_logger param to StreamListener.__init__, guarded DEBUG-level trace logs in receive() (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk) and flush() (flush trace), plus reset-state trace when allow_reuse=True. Only touching this one file.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implementing idle_timeout_s / tick() heartbeat feature: adding idle_timeout_s param and self._last_chunk_ts to StreamListener.__init__, updating receive() to refresh _last_chunk_ts on non-empty chunk, adding new tick(now_ts=None) method that emits StreamResponse(chunk='') on idle timeout and resets listener state unconditionally. Only touching streaming_listener.py.

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both claimed simultaneously - resolving conflict: I'll own the file (my CLAIM was sent first). Please DEFER dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py and send your SPEC for the debug logging feature (debug flag, debug_logger param, exact log call sites/messages/levels) so I implement both features together.

type: DEFER
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
spec: Add opt-in debug tracing to StreamListener. Implement exactly as follows:

1. __init__ signature: add two new keyword params at the end:
   debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None
   Store: self.debug = debug
   self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")
   (add `import logging` at top of file)

2. Add a small helper method for safe truncation, e.g.:
   def _preview(self, text: str, limit: int = 80) -> str:
       if text is None:
           return ""
       if len(text) <= limit:
           return text
       return text[:limit] + "..."
   Must NOT append "..." when len(text) <= limit.

3. Guard every debug log with: if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): ...
   Use self._logger.debug(...) — compute message/format args only inside the guard (no eager string building outside it).

4. Trace point - start detection: in receive(), right after the branch that sets self.stream_start = True inside the "we have a full identifier" case (after chunk_message is computed/adjusted, i.e. after the JSONAdapter leading-quote strip), log:
   f"Start detection: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', stream_start=True, buffer_preview='{self._preview(chunk_message)}'"
   Example outputs: "Start detection: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', stream_start=True, buffer_preview='\n'" and "Start detection: adapter=JSONAdapter, field='answer', stream_start=True, buffer_preview='Hello world!'"

5. Trace point - rolling end check: in receive(), inside `if self.stream_start:` block, right when `re.search(end_identifier, concat_message)` matches (end condition triggers, before/around self.stream_end = True), log:
   f"Rolling end check: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', reason='regex_match', buffered_size={len(concat_message)}"
   Example: "Rolling end check: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', reason='regex_match', buffered_size=25"

6. Trace point - emit chunk: right before `return StreamResponse(self.predict_name, self.signature_field_name, token)` at the end of receive(), log:
   f"Emit chunk: len(token)={len(token)}, queue_size={self.field_end_queue.qsize()}, is_last_chunk={self.stream_end}"
   Example: "Emit chunk: len(token)=12, queue_size=3, is_last_chunk=False"

7. Trace point - flush: in flush(), before returning (for every adapter branch, so easiest right before each return / or restructure to compute result then log then return), log:
   f"Flush: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', truncated_buffer_length={len(result)}"
   where adapter_name should be settings.adapter.__class__.__name__ if settings.adapter else "ChatAdapter", and result is the string about to be returned.
   Example: "Flush: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', truncated_buffer_length=15"

8. Trace point - state reset: in receive(), inside `if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse:` block, after clearing the state, log:
   f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)"
   Example: "State reset for field 'answer' (allow_reuse=True)"

All log messages use exact literal format/spacing/punctuation shown above (tests assert exact strings) - do not add extra text. No changes to detection algorithms, return values, or StreamResponse schema. No global logging config. This must not depend on or conflict with your idle_timeout_s/tick() feature - please place these debug log calls independent of your new tick() method logic (tick() doesn't need tracing per this spec)."

type: CLAIM

Existing tests/streaming/test_streaming.py pass (15 passed, 3 skipped). Done with my changes to this file.
